papers.name: Section I: Multiple-Choice Questions
papers.coverage_notes: 40 MCQ across 8 units. Scientific/graphing calculator allowed.

papers.name: Section II: Free-Response Questions
papers.coverage_notes: 4 FRQ: Q1 Mathematical Routines, Q2 Translation Between Representations, Q3 Experimental Design and Analysis, Q4 Qualitative/Quantitative Translation.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions, basic application.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when teacher/student/parent mode is declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: AP command words defined in the CED Section 2 'Exam Instructions'.
marking_guidance.notes: AP exams use a 5-point scoring scale (1-5). College credit typically awarded at 3+.
